key_findings.finding: Fabric writes MCP server entry to .claude/settings.json under mcpServers key — but Claude Code's documented MCP server file is ~/.claude.json (user) or .mcp.json (project root). settings.json is for hooks/permissions, not MCP server registration.

Fabric does NOT write a root-level CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or GEMINI.md. The bootstrap protocol writes only to .fabric/bootstrap/README.md, which is exposed to ALL clients via MCP resource (AGENTS_MD_RESOURCE_URI). The drift problem cited in research (CLAUDE.md ≠ AGENTS.md) is sidestepped by design — there is no parallel-file maintenance burden because Fabric does not own the project-root agent file.
MCP tool annotations: only readOnlyHint:true is declared; idempotentHint, destructiveHint:false, openWorldHint, and human-readable title are missing. Spec 2025-03-26 names all four annotations.
Codex TOML format compliance is exact: writes [mcp_servers.fabric] table at ~/.codex/config.toml; even migrates legacy [mcp.servers.fabric] block. Research-aligned.
Cursor writer compliant for project scope (.cursor/mcp.json) but does not offer user-scope (~/.cursor/mcp.json). Research lists both as valid.

MCP server has ZERO process signal handlers (no SIGINT/SIGTERM/SIGHUP). Research's pitfall #15945 (Claude Code zombie servers) is unmitigated. startStdioServer connects transport but never calls server.close() / transport.close() on shutdown.
evidence_refs: Grep for SIGINT|SIGTERM|SIGHUP|process.on across packages: no matches

key_findings.finding: MCP bootstrap README resource (AGENTS_MD_RESOURCE_URI) throws raw fs.readFile ENOENT errors directly back to the MCP client when the file is missing. Spec error code -32603 (internal) or a structured ResourceNotFound is appropriate, but the SDK only sees an unstructured Error.

HTTP API error normalizer uses fragile string-prefix matching for 3 specific messages. 'Cannot find human lock entry:' prefix is checked but NEVER thrown anywhere in packages/server (orphan branch — dead code, possible silent breakage if reintroduced with wording change).
evidence_refs: Grep 'Cannot find human lock entry' in packages: only at _error.ts:101

key_findings.finding: MCP tool input schemas use Zod (peer dep contract met). Output schemas are also declared via outputSchema parameter — better than spec minimum. However, schemas are inline-defined in tools/*.ts and NOT exported to packages/shared, so dashboard/clients cannot reuse them; high schema drift risk per research finding #38%.

Tool surface size: 2 tools registered in createFabricServer (fab_plan_context, fab_get_rule_sections). fab_get_rules is exported but NOT registered in the server. Well under Cursor 40-tool ceiling and the research-recommended ≤5 budget.

fab_get_rules tool is implemented and exported (packages/server/src/tools/get-rules.ts) but NEVER wired into createFabricServer — dead-code surface that diverges from spec-compliance promises if its protected token ('fabric_rules') is mentioned in user-facing strings without a backing tool.

Initialize/initialized handshake is delegated to @modelcontextprotocol/sdk McpServer. SDK handles the lifecycle correctly, so spec compliance for handshake is delegated and NOT batched (spec requires no batching of initialize). Capability declaration uses SDK defaults — if Fabric needs to advertise resources/tools, it does so implicitly via registerResource/registerTool calls.

ClientKind union (writer.ts L1-8) lists 7 clients. After scope reduction to Claude/Codex/Cursor, this is the typed source of truth that needs trimming to 4 (ClaudeCodeCLI, ClaudeCodeDesktop, Cursor, CodexCLI). TypeScript will surface every consumer.

fabric.config.json schema (3 sources of truth: types/config.ts, schemas/fabric-config.ts Zod, schemas/fabric-config.json JSON-Schema) all consistently list windsurf/rooCode/geminiCLI fields. JSON-Schema sets additionalProperties:false at root and clientPaths levels — so removing fields will reject existing user config files containing them.

agents_md_audit.drift_protection: By design, Fabric does not write any root-level agent file (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, GEMINI.md, .windsurfrules). The single source of truth is .fabric/bootstrap/README.md, exposed via MCP resource URI to all clients. The init-claude-install.test.ts (L71-73) is the regression guard asserting these files are NEVER created. AGENTS.md.template + framework variants exist under templates/agents-md/ but are read-only inputs, not write targets — they are templates the user can copy manually if they want a project-root AGENTS.md.
latent_bugs: GEMINI.md detection in resolver (L86, L111) and json.ts (L160) — used as a trigger to enable GeminiCLIWriter. After Gemini scope removal, this dual existence-check becomes dead code but is harmless until removed.

scanner/forensic.ts L1444 emits a Chinese recommendation referencing 'AGENTS.md 分层边界' — this is a user-facing string that survives scope reduction (talks about layered AGENTS.md, not a write target). No latent write bug, but recommendation text should stay aligned with what Fabric actually emits.

Cache invalidation comment at packages/server/src/cache.ts:91 says 'AGENTS.md may have changed' — this comment is talking about .fabric/agents/**/AGENTS.md (the rule taxonomy), NOT root-level. Easy to misread; suggest renaming the variable / clarifying the comment to 'rule files'.

client_config_audit.notes: MISMATCH: Fabric writes mcpServers.fabric into .claude/settings.json (packages/cli/src/config/json.ts:99-109). Per Claude Code docs, settings.json holds hooks/permissions; MCP servers belong in ~/.claude.json (user scope) or .mcp.json (project root, committable). Fabric's choice puts MCP registration in the wrong file. Symptom: users following Anthropic's docs to inspect/disable Fabric will look in the wrong place; Claude Code's published MCP loader may not even read mcpServers from settings.json (needs verification). Fabric ALSO writes hooks (Stop hook for agents-md-init-reminder) into settings.json, which IS a correct use of that file (init.ts:934-999). Fix: split into two writers — ClaudeCodeMcpWriter (writes .mcp.json or ~/.claude.json) and ClaudeCodeHooksWriter (settings.json).

client_config_audit.expected_path: ~/.codex/config.toml (user scope only — Codex CLI does not have a project-scope MCP file)
client_config_audit.notes: Format and path compliant. CodexTOMLConfigWriter.detect (toml.ts:95-103) only checks ~/.codex existence; no project-scope option. This matches research — Codex CLI is user-scope only. The writer also handles legacy [mcp.servers.fabric] migration to current [mcp_servers.fabric] (toml.ts:55-73), which is conservative and correct. Inline-table env serialization (toml.ts:29-35) is sorted-by-key, deterministic — good for snapshot tests.

Compliant for project scope. CursorWriter (json.ts:112-123) only emits the project-scope path; no user-scope option. This is acceptable because project scope is the recommended Cursor pattern, but a future enhancement could allow --scope=user. JSON shape (mcpServers.fabric = {command, args, env?}) matches Cursor docs exactly via shared writeJsonClientConfig helper (json.ts:49-60).

tool_annotation_audit.rationale: Read-only (does not mutate state); idempotent (same paths+intent yield same revision_hash and selection_token); openWorldHint=false (only reads .fabric/agents.meta.json under projectRoot — closed world). Title improves UX in clients that surface tool catalogs.

Same reasoning as fab_plan_context. Reads rule .md files only; does not write. Same selection_token returns same rules until revision changes. Note: this tool DOES emit Event Ledger records (correlation_id, session_id params) — that is a side effect, but appending audit logs is conventionally NOT considered destructive in MCP semantics. If strict, set readOnlyHint:false but mark idempotentHint:true.
Currently registered nowhere (orphan tool). Annotations are correct but moot until tools/get-rules.ts is wired into createFabricServer (or removed).

error_mapping_audit.should_be: Either remove (orphan — no thrower) OR convert to typed HumanLockEntryNotFoundError class with explicit error.code/error.status fields, mirroring AgentsMetaFileMissingError pattern (L76-82).
error_mapping_audit.rationale: No throw site found in packages/server/src for 'Cannot find human lock entry:'. This branch is dead code OR the throw site is missing. Either way, string-prefix matching is brittle (research's pattern: any rewording silently degrades to 500).

error_mapping_audit.should_be: Convert to typed LedgerEntryNotFoundError class. Existing throw site at packages/server/src/services/annotate-intent.ts:23 throws Error directly with that exact prefix.
error_mapping_audit.rationale: The throw site exists (annotate-intent.ts:23) but uses anonymous Error. Spec maps to MCP -32602 (invalid params: caller specified a non-existent ledger_entry_id). HTTP API gives 404, which is the correct REST mapping. Fix: introduce LedgerEntryNotFoundError extending Error with code/status, throw it from annotate-intent.ts, simplify _error.ts to instanceof check.

error_mapping_audit.should_be: Convert to PathOutsideProjectRootError class. Throw site at packages/server/src/services/_shared.ts:51.
error_mapping_audit.rationale: Same brittleness pattern. Should follow AgentsMetaFileMissingError typed-class pattern.

error_mapping_audit.current_code: throw new Error('selection_token is missing or expired') etc — all anonymous Error
error_mapping_audit.should_be: MCP -32602 (invalid params) for token/selection issues. Currently surfaces as unstructured Error to MCP client; SDK will likely wrap as -32603 internal.
error_mapping_audit.rationale: Per spec 2025-11-25, invalid client-provided params should return -32602. These throws indicate caller passed an invalid selection_token / stable_id / missing reason — all client-input-validation failures. Define InvalidSelectionTokenError, InvalidL1RuleSelectionError, MissingSelectionReasonError extending Error with code: -32602.

error_mapping_audit.current_code: throw new Error(`Target directory does not exist: ${projectRoot}`)
error_mapping_audit.should_be: MCP -32603 (internal) — server misconfiguration, not caller fault. Currently anonymous.
error_mapping_audit.rationale: If the project root doesn't exist when the server starts a tool call, that's a server-side problem (caller cannot fix). Anonymous Error is at least mapped to -32603 by SDK, but a typed ProjectRootMissingError improves clarity.

error_mapping_audit.current_code: Resource handler throws raw fs.readFile ENOENT (no try/catch, no mapping)
error_mapping_audit.should_be: Map ENOENT to MCP ResourceNotFoundError equivalent — current SDK exposes this via the registerResource handler returning a structured error or throwing an Error with .code:'ResourceNotFound'.
error_mapping_audit.rationale: If .fabric/bootstrap/README.md is absent, MCP client receives raw 'ENOENT: ...' which is opaque. Wrap in try/catch; on ENOENT throw new Error with descriptive message and (if SDK supports) structured code.

error_mapping_audit.current_code: parseEventLedgerLine returns null on parse failure (silent drop)
error_mapping_audit.should_be: Surface parse errors via doctor's createEventLedgerCheck; do not throw at parse time, but emit a parseable=false signal that doctor reports.
error_mapping_audit.rationale: Already handled by doctor.ts:431 parseable check. The smell is the dropped signal — invalid lines vanish from any consumer that doesn't run doctor. Acceptable for read paths, but document the contract.

handshake_lifecycle_audit.initialize_handler_evidence: Delegated to @modelcontextprotocol/sdk McpServer (packages/server/src/index.ts:59). SDK handles initialize → response → initialized exchange per spec 2025-11-25.
handshake_lifecycle_audit.capability_declaration: Implicit via registerTool (fab_plan_context, fab_get_rule_sections) and registerResource (AGENTS_MD_RESOURCE_URI). SDK auto-emits server capabilities = { tools, resources }. No explicit prompts/sampling capabilities — correct because Fabric does not implement them.
handshake_lifecycle_audit.signal_handler_evidence: Grep for SIGINT|SIGTERM|SIGHUP|process.on across packages/: NO MATCHES. Server relies on parent (Claude/Codex/Cursor) to terminate the child process. This matches the zombie-server pattern reported in claude-code issue #15945.
handshake_lifecycle_audit.shutdown_drain_evidence: startStdioServer (index.ts:92-97) connects transport but never registers shutdown logic. startHttpServer (index.ts:99-125) registers .once('close') for app.dispose() but not for parent SIGINT.
handshake_lifecycle_audit.leakage_risk: MEDIUM-HIGH. With ~/.claude/settings.json restart cycles or Cursor MCP refresh, the parent client may abandon the stdio child without sending SIGTERM cleanly. Without handlers, in-flight tool calls are not drained; .fabric/events.jsonl appendFile may leave a partial line (research's fsyncgate pitfall). Mitigation: (1) install SIGINT/SIGTERM/SIGHUP handlers in startStdioServer that call server.close()/transport.close(); (2) wrap appendFile in atomic-flush + rotation; (3) document a max-shutdown-deadline (e.g., 5s) before process.exit.
additional_lifecycle_gaps: No explicit transport.close() call on stdio EOF — SDK may handle this, but not verified by any test.
No top-level uncaughtException / unhandledRejection handlers — a poisoned ledger or schema crash could crash the server with no diagnostic emitted to stderr beyond formatError IIFE catch (L132-135).
Resource handler at index.ts:74-86 has no error boundary; thrown errors propagate to MCP client raw.
No version negotiation logic visible — relies entirely on SDK. Acceptable, but if client sends unsupported protocol version, behavior is SDK-defined.

tool_surface_count_detail.comment: Well within budget. fab_get_rules is orphan — wire it in or delete. The 2 registered tools form a coherent narrative (plan → fetch) which is good MCP UX.

schema_locations: packages/shared/src/schemas/fabric-config.ts (clientPathsSchema, fabricConfigSchema — different domain: workspace config, not MCP tool I/O)
schema_drift_surface.shared_export_detail: MCP tool schemas (plan-context, rule-sections, get-rules) are inline-defined in each tool file; NONE are exported to packages/shared. Dashboard / clients cannot import them; consumers re-derive types or stringly-type. This is the #1 drift vector per research's 38% MCP failure finding.
schema_drift_surface.backward_compat_evidence: No vitest snapshot test asserts the SDK-emitted JSON Schema for any of the 3 tools. No golden-file fixture per research recommendation. CI lint-protected-tokens guards string identifiers but NOT schema shape.
recommended_fixes: Move inputSchema + outputSchema definitions to packages/shared/src/schemas/mcp-tools.ts; export named (e.g., planContextInputSchema, planContextOutputSchema); import in tools/*.ts.

Add packages/server/test/tool-schema-snapshot.test.ts that imports each tool's schema, calls zodToJsonSchema (or use SDK's emitted schema via server.tool(...)?.toJSON()), and snapshots to __snapshots__/tool-schema.snap. CI fails on drift.
Add a contract test that boots a fake MCP client (using SDK's ClientStdioTransport to a mock pair), calls tools/list, and asserts the returned schema matches the committed snapshot byte-for-byte.

questions_for_user: 确认 Claude Code 的 MCP server 注册路径：研究文档说 ~/.claude.json（user）或 .mcp.json（project root），但代码写的是 .claude/settings.json。是否要在 v1.7.0 稳定化中拆分 ClaudeCodeCLIWriter 为两个 writer（一个写 .mcp.json 处理 MCP，另一个保留 settings.json 写 hooks）？这是 spec-compliance bug，但拆分会改变用户文件布局，需要用户确认是否在 minor 版本里推出。

对 fab_get_rules 工具：它在 packages/server/src/tools/get-rules.ts 中实现并 export，但 createFabricServer 没有注册它。是 (a) 注册它（恢复成 3-tool 工具集，仍 ≤5），(b) 删除整个 get-rules.ts（清理孤儿代码），还是 (c) 保留导出供 HTTP API 使用但不注册到 MCP？历史决策不明，需要项目所有者澄清。
MCP server 是否要在 v1.7.0 稳定化里加 SIGINT/SIGTERM/SIGHUP handlers？研究指出这是 Claude Code zombie-server 问题（issue #15945）的根因，无 handler 时父进程异常退出会留下子进程。修复成本很小（~30 行），收益是 production stability。
MCP tool annotations 是否升级到 spec 2025-03-26 完整集（destructiveHint:false / openWorldHint:false / title）？目前只有 readOnlyHint:true。改动小，提升 Cursor / Claude 自动批准 UX。
_error.ts 字符串前缀匹配是否要重构为 typed error classes？这个重构碰到 packages/server/src/api/* 的多个文件，但能消除研究 pitfall（rewording silently degrades to 500）和 dead-code 'Cannot find human lock entry' 分支。
MCP tool input/output schema 是否要 export 到 packages/shared 并加 snapshot 契约测试？这是研究 pitfall #38% schema drift 的直接对应。代价：schema 文件搬家 + 1 个新测试文件；收益：CI 拦住 schema 回归。
Cursor user-scope 配置（~/.cursor/mcp.json）是否要支持？目前只写 project-scope，但有的 monorepo 用户可能希望 user-scope 一次配置全部 workspace。低优先级，但 v1.7.0 稳定化窗口可以一并处理。

_metadata.perspective_focus: MCP spec / AGENTS.md / client config formats
_metadata.audit_method: Read all server tool files, all CLI config writers, error normalizer, init.ts plan execution, init-claude-install.test.ts; ripgrep for AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md/SIGINT/throw-sites cross-package; cross-checked actual paths against research's documented client paths.
spec_versions_referenced: Cursor MCP docs (JSON at ~/.cursor/mcp.json or .cursor/mcp.json; 40-tool ceiling)

partial: Schema not exported / no drift tests
noncompliant: Claude MCP write target (.claude/settings.json instead of .mcp.json or ~/.claude.json)

_metadata.confidence: high — all findings are line-cited; audit covers 100% of MCP tool files, 100% of config writers, error normalizer, and stdio entry point.
